title.en: Taking a Taxi in Korea: Destination & Requests

description.en: Don't be nervous in a taxi! Learn the polite way to state your destination and how to ask the driver to pull over with phrases like "여기서 내려주세요."
description.ko: 택시를 탔을 때 당황하지 않고 목적지를 말하는 법! 그리고 원하는 위치에 정확하고 정중하게 내려달라고 요청하는 리액션을 배워봅니다.

tags: Situation. Travel. Taxi. Transportation. Request. Location. Season 2

content.speaker: John
content.text: Mina, taking a taxi in Korea is a bit intimidating for me.

content.speaker: John
content.text: The drivers move so fast, and I'm always afraid I'll miss my stop because I don't know what to say!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 걱정 마세요 존! 택시 기사님들은 짧고 명확한 리액션을 좋아하시거든요.
content.translation: Haha, don't worry John! Taxi drivers like short and clear reactions.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 우선 기사님을 부를 때는..., "기사님" 이라고 부르는 게 가장 정중해요.
content.translation: First, when calling the driver, it's most polite to call them "Gisanim".

content.speaker: John
content.text: 기사님. It sounds much more respectful than just saying "Mr. Driver," right?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요! 그리고 내리고 싶은 곳 근처에 왔을 때 쓰는 마법의 문장이 있어요.
content.translation: Right! And there's a magic sentence to use when you're near where you want to get off.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 바로 "여기서 내려주세요" 라고 말하는거예요. 오늘은 택시 안에서 당황하지 않는 필살 리액션을 배워볼게요!
content.translation: It's "Yeogiseo naeryeojuseyo." Today, we'll learn the "must-know" reactions so you don't panic inside a taxi!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 어디로 가주세요. This is how I tell them my destination, right?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네! 장소 뒤에..., "어디로 가주세요" 만 붙이면 돼요.
content.translation: Yes! Just add "~ro gajuseyo" after the location.

insight.title: ~(으)로 가주세요
insight.usage_tip: Location + '으로' (if ends in consonant) or '로' (if ends in vowel) + '가주세요'. This is the standard way to state your destination.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 홍대역으로 가주세요.
content.translation: Please go to Hongdae Station.

content.speaker: John
content.text: And what about the magic stop phrase?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 땐..., "여기서 내려주세요" 라고 하세요.
content.translation: In that case, say "Yeogiseo naeryeojuseyo."

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "여기" 대신에..., "저기" 또는..., "저 편의점 앞" 처럼 구체적인 장소를 넣어도 좋아요.
content.translation: Instead of "here," you can also put a specific place like "over there" or "in front of that convenience store."

insight.title: 여기서 내려주세요
insight.usage_tip: '여기(Here)' + '서(At)' + '내려주세요(Please let me down)'. Use this about 10-20 meters before you actually want to stop.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay, let's jump in. I'm getting into a taxi and I want to go to Gangnam Station.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 안녕하세요! 기사님. 강남역으로 가주세요.
content.translation: Hello! Driver, please go to Gangnam Station.

insight.title: 가주세요
insight.usage_tip: Using a clear, confident voice is helpful in a noisy taxi.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네. 강남역요? 알겠습니다.
content.translation: Yes, Gangnam Station? Got it.

content.speaker: John
content.emotion: 창밖을 보다가 목적지 근처에 도착했을 때
content.text: 아, 기사님! 저기 편의점 앞에서 내려주세요.
content.translation: Ah, driver! Please drop me off in front of that convenience store over there.

insight.title: 저기 ~ 앞에서 내려주세요
insight.usage_tip: Giving a landmark like a convenience store (편의점) helps the driver find a safe spot to stop.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네. 여기서 세워드릴게요.
content.translation: Yes, I'll pull over here.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 감사합니다. 수고하세요!
content.translation: Thank you. Driver, keep up the good work!

insight.title: 수고하세요
insight.usage_tip: This is a very common and warm way to say goodbye to someone who is working.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Wow, it’s so simple! I just need to remember "기사님" and "내려주세요."

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 그리고 내릴 때 "수고하세요" 라고 인사하면 기사님도 정말 기분 좋아하실 거예요.
content.translation: Right. And if you greet them with "Sugo-haseyo" when you get out, the driver will feel really good too.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "taxi" reactions one last time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 강남역으로 가주세요!
content.translation: Please go to [Location]!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 여기서 내려주세요!
content.translation: Please drop me off here!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 저기 앞에서 세워주세요!
content.translation: Please pull over in front of there!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 수고하세요!
content.translation: Keep up the good work! / Goodbye!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 영수증 주시겠어요?
content.translation: Could I have a receipt?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 오늘 택시를 탄다면, 당황하지 말고 "기사님!" 하고 반갑게 불러보세요.
content.translation: Everyone, if you take a taxi today, don't panic and call out "Gisanim!" brightly.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "여기서 내려주세요!"
content.translation: Please drop me off here!

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you in the next episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 택시 기사님들은 길을 정말 잘 아세요. (Taxi drivers know the roads really well.)
택시를 타기 전에 목적지를 미리 확인하세요. (Check your destination before taking a taxi.)

vocabulary.meaning: To get off / To drop off
vocabulary.example: 다음 정류장에서 내릴게요. (I'll get off at the next stop.)
저기 건물 앞에 차를 세워주세요. (Please stop the car in front of that building.)
한국에는 어디에나 편의점이 있어서 편리해요. (It's convenient because there are convenience stores everywhere in Korea.)

vocabulary.meaning: To work hard / To put in effort
vocabulary.example: 오늘도 수고한 당신, 푹 쉬세요! (You worked hard today, get some good rest!)
